title: BNCI 2020-001 Reach-and-Grasp Electrode Comparison EEG dataset
description: This EEG dataset comprises recordings from 45 healthy participants performing self-initiated reach-and-grasp motor imagery tasks using three different electrode systems: gel-based laboratory equipment (g.tec USBamp/g.tec Ladybird), water-based mobile EEG (EEG-Versatile), and dry-electrode mobile EEG (EEG-Hero). Participants executed palmar and lateral grasp actions toward objects, with 58 EEG channels sampled at 256 Hz. The dataset enables comparative evaluation of neural correlates of natural reach-and-grasp actions across recording modalities and assessment of mobile EEG systems for brain-computer interface applications.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired at 256 Hz from 58 channels using three systems: gel-based active electrodes (g.tec USBamp/g.tec Ladybird, reference: right earlobe, ground: AFz), water-based mobile EEG (EEG-Versatile), and dry-electrode mobile EEG (EEG-Hero). Online filtering included 8th order Chebyshev filter (0.01-100 Hz) and 50 Hz notch filter. Participants performed 80 self-initiated reach-and-grasp trials each toward a glass (palmar grasp) and spoon (lateral grasp) in self-paced mode with visual feedback. Offline preprocessing included 4th order Butterworth bandpass filtering (0.3-60 Hz), extended infomax ICA for artifact removal (gel-based and water-based systems), amplitude thresholding (>125 µV), abnormal joint probability (4 SD), and abnormal kurtosis (4 SD) rejection. Data were re-referenced to common average reference and downsampled to 16 Hz.
